data.title: As a developer, the ticket CLI dispatcher init runs without spawning Python processes, with a rollback flag for safety
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Replace `_ensure_initialized`'s two Python processes (path canonicalization + sync-marker hash) with bash-native equivalents. Preserve the existing `/tmp/.ticket-sync-<hash>` marker filename scheme byte-for-byte by keeping the same hash algorithm (md5, 12-hex-char truncation) currently used at `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket:69`. Introduce a `DSO_TICKET_LEGACY=1` env flag that bypasses the bash-native path and falls back to the pre-refactor Python logic for the first release cycle.

**Why**: The two Python processes account for ~160–300ms of every ticket CLI call. Replacing them with bash-native path canonicalization and hashing is the single biggest single-story latency win. The rollback flag is a safety net — if a corner-case bug surfaces post-release, users can set the env var and continue working while a fix is developed.

**Scope**:
- IN: Bash-native path canonicalization (matches current Python `os.path.realpath` semantics for symlinks, relative segments, missing components); bash-native sync-marker hash computation using **md5** truncated to 12 hex chars (verified against current Python output bit-for-bit); `DSO_TICKET_LEGACY=1` env-var check that skips the bash-native path; docs entry in CLAUDE.md quick reference describing the flag and its lifetime.
- OUT: `write_commit_event` rehome (Story C); library file `ticket-lib-api.sh` (Story B); library extension pattern (Story D); platform-matrix testing (Story F).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `_ensure_initialized` spawns zero `python3` processes on the default (non-legacy) path, verified by `tests/scripts/test-ticket-subprocess-count.sh` or an extension of it
  ← Satisfies: "The CLI dispatcher sources the library and delegates all covered operations to library functions"
- When this story is complete, the bash-native sync-marker hash produces the **same** `/tmp/.ticket-sync-<hash>` filename as the current Python implementation for an identical canonical tracker path (md5, 12-hex truncation) — verified by a direct byte-compare test
  ← Satisfies: "The library operates correctly across platforms ... tickets containing shell-special characters ... round-trip correctly"
- When this story is complete, setting `DSO_TICKET_LEGACY=1` restores the pre-refactor Python-based `_ensure_initialized` behavior — verified by an integration test that toggles the flag and asserts the Python code paths execute
  ← Satisfies: "A `DSO_TICKET_LEGACY=1` env flag reverts to the pre-refactor Python-backed path, available for at least the first release cycle after rollout"
- When this story is complete, read-path operations (`ticket show`, `ticket list`) complete in <0.15s per call on Linux reference hardware — verified via hyperfine against the A0 baseline
  ← Satisfies: "Read-path operations (ticket show, list) complete in <0.15s per call"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Performance] Must shave ~160–300ms from every call on Linux reference hardware; validated by comparing to the A0 baseline
- [Reliability] Sync-marker hash MUST be cross-process compatible with the existing `/tmp/.ticket-sync-*` marker file format so legacy-flag flips do not trigger spurious re-initialization

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
